title: TYPING_NOTIFY in relay.rs ignores relay-provided chat_id; uses daemon default_channel_id instead
description: daemon/src/relay.rs TYPING_NOTIFY handler: let chat_id = state.default_channel_id().unwrap_or('default').to_string(). The TypingNotifyParams has no chat_id field at this level, but the relay broadcasts typing on a per-channel basis. The daemon hardwires the typing event to the default channel, so a typing indicator from any channel appears in only one channel. When multiple channels exist (e.g. spaces with separate channels), typing indicators from non-default channels are misrouted. Compare with PeerTyping (in dispatch_peer_typing) which correctly uses the chat_id from the ClearMessage payload. These two paths diverge: direct relay typing always goes to default channel; MLS-encrypted peer typing goes to the correct channel.
